name: Novales, Comillas, Cabezón de la Sal – circular route
name_original: Novales, Comillas, Cabezón de la Sal – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Alfoz De Lloredo offers a diverse landscape on the western coast of Cantabria, Spain. The region features a mix of coastal areas with dramatic cliffs and beaches, alongside lush inland terrain characterized by forests and rolling hills. This varied topography provides routes with different elevations, from gentle coastal paths to more demanding climbs through forested areas. A unique microclimate in areas like Novales supports distinctive natural features such as lemon groves. Best road cycling routes around Alfoz De Lloredo The most popular road cycling route is Novales, Comillas, Cabezón de la Sal – circular route, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes through cultural landmarks. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Palacio de Sobrellano – Santillana del Mar loop from Caborredondo, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.9 km) path. This route connects historic towns and cultural sites. Local road cyclists also love the Oyambre Natural Park – Beautiful quiet road loop from Casar de Periedo, a 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail leading through Oyambre Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Alfoz De Lloredo is defined by coastal paths, forests, and rolling inland terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Alfoz De Lloredo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 6,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alfoz De Lloredo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: Alfoz de Lloredo offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 130 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: The region features a diverse landscape, combining dramatic coastal areas with lush inland forests and rolling hills. You'll find a mix of gentle coastal paths and more demanding climbs through forested areas, offering varied elevations for different cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: Yes, Alfoz de Lloredo has several easy road cycling routes suitable for families. These routes often feature less challenging terrain, allowing for a more relaxed ride. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' to find suitable options.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see while cycling in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: While cycling, you can explore a variety of natural and cultural highlights. The coastline boasts impressive cliffs like Bolao and Puerto Calderón, and beautiful beaches such as Luaña Beach. Inland, the village of Novales is known for its unique 'lemon oasis'. Cultural sites include the majestic 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/344751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas&lt;/a&gt;, home to Gaudí's El Capricho.

Q: Are there any specific climbs for experienced road cyclists in the region?
A: Yes, for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers specific climbs such as the Barrio Rodezas Climb and Ca 371 Climb, both categorized as Category 4. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park – Beautiful quiet road loop from Casar de Periedo&lt;/a&gt; also feature significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: Alfoz de Lloredo benefits from a favorable microclimate, making it suitable for cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes, while summer provides warmer weather for coastal rides.

Q: Where can I find parking for my bike when cycling in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages throughout Alfoz de Lloredo, including Novales, the municipal capital, and starting points for many routes. Look for designated parking areas in these locations, especially near popular trailheads.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists with bikes in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: While specific bus routes with bike racks may vary, public transport options to and from Alfoz de Lloredo are available. It's advisable to check with local bus operators regarding their policies for carrying bicycles, especially for routes connecting to larger towns like Santillana del Mar or Comillas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Alfoz de Lloredo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005124362/novales-comillas-cabezon-de-la-sal-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Novales, Comillas, Cabezón de la Sal – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38265327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Sobrellano – Santillana del Mar loop from Caborredondo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: The road cycling routes in Alfoz de Lloredo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, stunning coastal views, and the opportunity to pass through charming historic towns.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass through or near significant cultural and historical sites. You can cycle to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/344751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the area around the Abadía de Santa María de Viaceli in Cóbreces, known for its peaceful atmosphere.

Q: Where can cyclists find refreshments or accommodation in Alfoz de Lloredo?
A: The towns and villages within Alfoz de Lloredo, such as Novales, Cóbreces, and nearby Comillas and Santillana del Mar, offer various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. These places provide convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays for cyclists exploring the region.

Q: Are there any easy, flat road cycling routes for beginners?
A: While the region has varied terrain, there are easier routes available, particularly those closer to the coast or within flatter inland sections. These routes are ideal for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous ride. Look for routes marked as 'easy' in the guide.
tips: It enjoys a privileged location in a valley surrounded by mountains, with a microclimate that allows Mediterranean-type crops, particularly citrus, lemons from Novales being well known.

Spectacular views from the road towards the Zapedo marsh. One of the wetlands that form at the mouths of the estuaries in the Cantabrian Sea and are home to various marine species and aquatic birds. Many of them play a fundamental role in the town's economy.

The original Cistercian abbey is now a Trappist monastery dedicated to Santa Maria Viaceli. It was built in the neo-Gothic style (1906-1910). In 2013 it was declared an Asset of Local Interest in Cantabria. They sell the excellent Trappist cheese "Queso Trapa" in the little shop on the street.
